summary: A Python CLI tool that prints 'hello, world' to standard output when executed. The tool supports version query (--version), help display (--help), and defaults to printing the greeting message with no arguments. It uses only Python standard library, is compatible with Python 3.7+, and returns exit code 0 on success.

functional_requirements.title: Print Greeting Message
functional_requirements.description: The system shall print 'hello, world' to stdout when executed with no arguments or when the default action is invoked. The output must exactly match 'hello, world' with a trailing newline character.
acceptance_criteria: Running the tool with no arguments outputs 'hello, world' followed by a newline to stdout
The output string exactly matches 'hello, world' (case-sensitive, no extra whitespace or quotes)
The process exits with status code 0

functional_requirements.title: Display Version Information
functional_requirements.description: The system shall display its version number when invoked with --version or -V flag. The version follows semantic versioning format (e.g., 0.1.0).
acceptance_criteria: Running the tool with --version outputs the version string to stdout
Running the tool with -V produces the same output as --version
The process exits with status code 0

functional_requirements.title: Display Help Information
functional_requirements.description: The system shall display usage information including description, available arguments, and usage examples when invoked with --help or -h flag.
acceptance_criteria: Running the tool with --help outputs usage information to stdout
The help text includes tool description, argument list, and usage examples
Running the tool with -h produces the same output as --help
The process exits with status code 0

functional_requirements.title: Default No-Argument Behavior
functional_requirements.description: When invoked without any command-line arguments, the system shall execute the default action of printing 'hello, world' to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: Executing the tool with no arguments produces identical output to the default greeting action
No error messages or warnings are produced when running with no arguments
The process exits with status code 0

non_functional_requirements.title: Execution Efficiency
non_functional_requirements.description: The tool shall complete execution within 1 second. For the simple print operation, actual execution time should be well under 100 milliseconds. Memory usage must remain under 10 MB.

non_functional_requirements.title: Python Compatibility
non_functional_requirements.description: The tool shall be compatible with Python 3.7 and above, using only the standard library with no third-party dependencies. It must run on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

non_functional_requirements.title: Reliability
non_functional_requirements.description: The tool shall always produce the expected output without runtime errors, exceptions, or undefined behavior. Output encoding must be UTF-8.

non_functional_requirements.title: Maintainability
non_functional_requirements.description: Source code shall be concise (under 100 lines excluding comments and blank lines), follow PEP 8 style guidelines, and include necessary explanatory comments.

use_cases.goal: 运行工具并获取 hello, world 输出
main_flow: 用户在终端中执行工具命令
工具启动并执行默认逻辑
工具向 stdout 输出 hello, world
工具正常退出，返回状态码 0

use_cases.goal: 获取工具的版本号信息
main_flow: 用户在终端中执行工具并添加 --version 参数
工具解析参数并识别版本请求
工具向 stdout 输出版本号
工具正常退出，返回状态码 0

use_cases.goal: 查看工具的使用说明和参数信息
main_flow: 用户在终端中执行工具并添加 --help 参数
工具解析参数并识别帮助请求
工具向 stdout 输出帮助信息
工具正常退出，返回状态码 0
